requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement VX-SYS-401 and belongs to milestone PF2.0.
requirements.purpose: The VX-900 controller shall support dual CAN communication channels for operational and diagnostic communication.
requirements.inputs: The required input artifacts include ARXML communication configuration files and CAN database definitions.
requirements.outputs: The debug CAN interface shall broadcast diagnostic counters, fault identifiers, calibration parameters, and internal state-machine transitions.
requirements.process:
The process flow requires the following:
Apply HV battery voltage greater than 480V.
Wait 250 milliseconds after voltage stabilization.
Enable communication stack initialization.
Transmit operational enable command over MCAN.
requirements.validation: Validation activities shall be performed using CANalyzer and CANoe.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The acceptance criteria require all communication messages to remain within configured PDU timing tolerances.
requirements.external_events: Transmit operational enable command over MCAN.
requirements.constraints: The acceptance criteria require all communication messages to remain within configured PDU timing tolerances.
requirements.testability: Testable through: Validation activities shall be performed using CANalyzer and CANoe.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement VX-SYS-401 and belongs to milestone PF2.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_COMM_101 corresponds to customer requirement VX-SYS-401 and belongs to milestone PF2.0.
The VX-900 controller shall support dual CAN communication channels for operational and diagnostic communication.
MCAN communication shall handle vehicle operational messages while DCAN communication shall handle debugging and diagnostics.
The software shall transmit battery voltage, battery current, operating mode, thermal status, HVIL state, and fault levels through MCAN.
The debug CAN interface shall broadcast diagnostic counters, fault identifiers, calibration parameters, and internal state-machine transitions.
The required input artifacts include ARXML communication configuration files and CAN database definitions.
The process flow requires the following:
Apply HV battery voltage greater than 480V.
Wait 250 milliseconds after voltage stabilization.
Enable communication stack initialization.
Transmit operational enable command over MCAN.
The preloaded conditions require:
System mode = Standby
Fault status = No Fault
HVIL = Closed
The requirement priority is Medium.
Validation activities shall be performed using CANalyzer and CANoe.
The requirement is considered feasible within all opera

Transmit operational enable command over MCAN.

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement VX-SYS-402 and belongs to milestone PF2.1.
requirements.purpose: The VX-900 controller shall support Signal Not Available handling for all CAN signals.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Accepted when The VX-900 controller shall support Signal Not Available handling for all CAN signals is demonstrated under the specified operating conditions while satisfying the stated limits: For physical quantities, SNA shall use the maximum representable signal value.
requirements.valid_range_of_values:
For physical quantities, SNA shall use the maximum representable signal value.
The valid battery voltage range is 180V through 920V.
The valid current range is -250A through 250A.
requirements.data_latency_period: Whenever signal timeout exceeds 120 milliseconds, the signal shall transition into SNA state.
requirements.external_events:
Whenever signal timeout exceeds 120 milliseconds, the signal shall transition into SNA state.
Operation Command Requirement
requirements.temporal_events: Whenever signal timeout exceeds 120 milliseconds, the signal shall transition into SNA state.
requirements.constraints: The valid battery voltage range is 180V through 920V.
requirements.effects_on_other_systems: The default startup condition requires FaultStatus equal to No Fault.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement VX-SYS-402 and belongs to milestone PF2.1.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_COMM_102 corresponds to customer requirement VX-SYS-402 and belongs to milestone PF2.1.
The VX-900 controller shall support Signal Not Available handling for all CAN signals.
Whenever signal timeout exceeds 120 milliseconds, the signal shall transition into SNA state.
For physical quantities, SNA shall use the maximum representable signal value.
For state-based signals, SNA shall use INVALID state encoding.
The valid battery voltage range is 180V through 920V.
The valid current range is -250A through 250A.
The default startup condition requires FaultStatus equal to No Fault.
The validation environment shall include UART logging and CAN trace monitoring.
This requirement is classified as High priority and Critical.
Operation Command Requirement

requirements.purpose: The software shall support the following operating states:
requirements.process: The process sequence for entering Normal Operation is:

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement VX-SYS-510 and belongs to milestone B-Sample/PF3.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_STATE_210 corresponds to customer requirement VX-SYS-510 and belongs to milestone B-Sample/PF3.0.
The software shall support the following operating states:
Standby
Initialization
Precharge
Normal Operation
Safe State
Shutdown
Fault State
The software shall receive operation commands over CAN communication.
Supported command values are:
0x00 = Standby
0x01 = Enable
0x02 = Safe State
0x03 = Shutdown
The process sequence for entering Normal Operation is:
Verify ignition ON condition.
Verify HVIL closed state.
Verify no active Level 2 faults.
Enable precharge path.
Verify DC-link stabilization.
Enable main power stage.
The preloaded condition requires HV battery voltage greater than 450V.
The software shall reject invalid state transitions.
The validation activities require HIL simulation and fault-injection testing.
Overvoltage Diagnostic Requirement

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement VX-DIAG-801 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
requirements.purpose: The controller shall detect HV bus overvoltage conditions using ADC measurements.
requirements.process:
The process flow includes:
Read ADC measurement.
requirements.validation:
Validate debounce timer.
Validation testing shall include simulated ADC injection testing and CAN fault verification.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Accepted when The controller shall detect HV bus overvoltage conditions using ADC measurements is demonstrated under the specified operating conditions while satisfying the stated limits: The valid voltage operating range is 250V to 950V.
requirements.valid_range_of_values: The valid voltage operating range is 250V to 950V.
requirements.data_latency_period: Whenever measured HV voltage exceeds 930V for more than 40 milliseconds, the software shall trigger a Level 2 fault.
requirements.temporal_events: Whenever measured HV voltage exceeds 930V for more than 40 milliseconds, the software shall trigger a Level 2 fault.
requirements.constraints: The valid voltage operating range is 250V to 950V.
requirements.effects_on_other_systems:
The output response requires:
broadcasting Level 2 fault status over CAN.
Broadcast fault state.
The default condition requires OverVoltageFault equal to FALSE.
requirements.testability:
Testable through: Validate debounce timer.
Validation testing shall include simulated ADC injection testing and CAN fault verification.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement VX-DIAG-801 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_DIAG_310 corresponds to customer requirement VX-DIAG-801 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
The controller shall detect HV bus overvoltage conditions using ADC measurements.
Whenever measured HV voltage exceeds 930V for more than 40 milliseconds, the software shall trigger a Level 2 fault.
The output response requires:
disabling the power stage,
opening HV contactors,
broadcasting Level 2 fault status over CAN.
The process flow includes:
Read ADC measurement.
Compare voltage against threshold.
Validate debounce timer.
Trigger fault manager.
Broadcast fault state.
The default condition requires OverVoltageFault equal to FALSE.
The valid voltage operating range is 250V to 950V.
Validation testing shall include simulated ADC injection testing and CAN fault verification.
The requirement priority is High.
Thermal Protection Requirement

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement VX-DIAG-802 and belongs to milestone PF3.1.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_DIAG_311 corresponds to customer requirement VX-DIAG-802 and belongs to milestone PF3.1.
The software shall continuously monitor thermal sensors connected through SPI ADC interfaces.
The monitored components include:
Main Power Module
HV EMI Filter
LV EMI Filter
Solid-State Switch
Cooling Interface
Whenever temperature exceeds 88 degrees Celsius, the software shall activate thermal derating.
Whenever temperature exceeds 96 degrees Celsius, the software shall initiate controlled shutdown.
The ADC sampling interval shall be 10 milliseconds.
Thermal warnings shall be broadcast over CAN communication.
The valid operating temperature range is -40C to 125C.
Thermal chamber testing shall be used for validation.
The Critical field is intentionally unspecified.
Sensor Interface Requirement

The Critical field is intentionally unspecified.

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement VX-SENSOR-901 and belongs to milestone PF4.0.
requirements.purpose: The VX-900 controller shall acquire current, voltage, and temperature measurements through SPI ADC interfaces.
requirements.validation:
The software shall validate ADC ranges before processing measurement values.
Oscilloscope verification and ADC signal injection testing shall be used during validation.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Accepted when The VX-900 controller shall acquire current, voltage, and temperature measurements through SPI ADC interfaces is demonstrated under the specified operating conditions while satisfying the stated limits: The valid voltage range is 0V to 1000V.
requirements.valid_range_of_values:
The valid voltage range is 0V to 1000V.
The valid current range is -300A to 300A.
requirements.data_latency_period: The ADC sampling period shall be 5 milliseconds.
requirements.data_rate: The ADC sampling period shall be 5 milliseconds.
requirements.temporal_events: The ADC sampling period shall be 5 milliseconds.
requirements.constraints: The software shall validate ADC ranges before processing measurement values.
requirements.testability:
Testable through: The software shall validate ADC ranges before processing measurement values.
Oscilloscope verification and ADC signal injection testing shall be used during validation.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement VX-SENSOR-901 and belongs to milestone PF4.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_SENSOR_401 corresponds to customer requirement VX-SENSOR-901 and belongs to milestone PF4.0.
The VX-900 controller shall acquire current, voltage, and temperature measurements through SPI ADC interfaces.
The ADC sampling period shall be 5 milliseconds.
The monitored signals include:
HV Bus Voltage
LV Bus Voltage
HV Bus Current
LV Bus Current
Power Module Temperature
EMI Filter Temperature
The software shall validate ADC ranges before processing measurement values.
The measurement outputs shall be filtered before transmission to upper software layers.
The valid voltage range is 0V to 1000V.
The valid current range is -300A to 300A.
Oscilloscope verification and ADC signal injection testing shall be used during validation.
OBD Diagnostic Requirement

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement VX-OBD-1101 and belongs to milestone PF4.1.
requirements.purpose: The software shall support OBD Mode 4 diagnostic clearing requests.
requirements.process:
The process flow includes:
Receive Mode 4 request.
requirements.validation:
Validate session permissions.
UDS diagnostic testing and CAN trace verification shall be used for validation.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Accepted when The software shall support OBD Mode 4 diagnostic clearing requests is demonstrated under the specified operating conditions.
requirements.data_retention_period: Whenever a valid Mode 4 request is received, the software shall clear active DTCs and update nonvolatile memory.
requirements.testability:
Testable through: Validate session permissions.
UDS diagnostic testing and CAN trace verification shall be used for validation.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement VX-OBD-1101 and belongs to milestone PF4.1.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_OBD_510 corresponds to customer requirement VX-OBD-1101 and belongs to milestone PF4.1.
The software shall support OBD Mode 4 diagnostic clearing requests.
Whenever a valid Mode 4 request is received, the software shall clear active DTCs and update nonvolatile memory.
The software shall reject unauthorized diagnostic sessions.
The process flow includes:
Receive Mode 4 request.
Validate session permissions.
Clear active DTCs.
Update NVM storage.
Broadcast updated diagnostic status.
UDS diagnostic testing and CAN trace verification shall be used for validation.
The requirement is classified as Critical.
Non-Functional Requirement

requirements.purpose: The software startup time shall remain below 700 milliseconds after ignition ON. CPU utilization shall remain below 72 percent during normal operation. Flash memory utilization shall remain below 85 percent.
requirements.validation:
Long-duration stress testing and runtime profiling shall be used for validation.
Some deployment constraints are intentionally omitted from this document for testing purposes.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Accepted when The software startup time shall remain below 700 milliseconds after ignition ON. CPU utilization shall remain below 72 percent during normal operation. Flash memory utilization shall remain below 85 percent is demonstrated under the specified operating conditions.
requirements.data_latency_period: The software startup time shall remain below 700 milliseconds after ignition ON.
requirements.external_events: The software startup time shall remain below 700 milliseconds after ignition ON.
requirements.temporal_events: The software startup time shall remain below 700 milliseconds after ignition ON.
requirements.testability:
Testable through: Long-duration stress testing and runtime profiling shall be used for validation.
Some deployment constraints are intentionally omitted from this document for testing purposes.

metadata.evidence:
NFR-1501 and belongs to milestone Release 2.0.
The software startup time shall remain below 700 milliseconds after ignition ON.
CPU utilization shall remain below 72 percent during normal operation.
Flash memory utilization shall remain below 85 percent.
The software shall support operating temperatures between -40C and 105C.
Long-duration stress testing and runtime profiling shall be used for validation.
The requirement priority is Medium.
Some deployment constraints are intentionally omitted from this document for testing purposes.
